verifier_values.criteria: States the patient has non-small cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the OpenEMR patient chart (Onc Patient 7), tab Clinical Notes, row 33, column E, which says, "stage I non-small cell lung cancer." This also requires synthesizing the statement from the script, which says, "I’m sorry the lung cancer you developed several years ago has returned. It is the same type as before." Putting both together means this patient has non-small cell lung cancer. 
The script also says. "We know that it is non-squamous, but we need more information." and that it is metastatic "Now, that same cancer has spread to some lymph nodes and a small spot in the opposite lung."

verifier_values.criteria: States the patient's cancer is metastatic.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: The script says. "We know that it is non-squamous, but we need more information." and that it is metastatic "Now, that same cancer has spread to some lymph nodes and a small spot in the opposite lung."

verifier_values.criteria: States the patient's cancer has a ROS1 mutation.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient lab results. "ROS1 Rearrangement Positive." "G2032R."

verifier_values.criteria: States that talectrinib is effective against G2032R mutations.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Talectrinib has been shown to be effective against G2032R mutations. "Taletrectinib exhibits in vivo activity in models harboring ROS1 wild-type (ROS1-CD74, ROS1-SLC34A2) and ROS1-G2032R resistance mutation fusions." https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/85/8_Supplement_1/5612/758377/Abstract-5612-Taletrectinib-a-next-generation

verifier_values.criteria: States talectrinib is U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved for metastatic ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
"On June 11, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration approved taletrectinib (Ibtrozi, Nuvation Bio Inc.), a kinase inhibitor, for adults with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC)."

https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-approves-taletrectinib-ros1-positive-non-small-cell-lung-cancer

verifier_values.criteria: States the most appropriate next treatment option is talectrinib.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient meets all of the indications to start talectrinib. "Talectrinib is a kinase inhibitor indicated for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219713s000lbl.pdf
It is also superior to lorlatinib as it is more suitable in TKI-naive patients.

verifier_values.criteria: States that talectrinib is dosed once daily.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is the frequency of talectrinib . "Recommended Dosage: 600 mg orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219713s000lbl.pdf
This is suitable for the patient who wants to take as little pills as possible as per the script: ""I’ve noticed I’ve been getting pretty forgetful. I want to take as few pills as possible."

verifier_values.criteria: States that talectrinib is dosed at 600 mg.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the dosage of talectrinib . "Recommended Dosage: 600 mg orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219713s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that talectrinib is dosed orally.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the route of administration of talectrinib. "Recommended Dosage: 600 mg orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219713s000lbl.pdf
